mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction amid rising costs, forcing equity markets to price in corporate margin compression and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises and RSAFS falls, with a combined Rank IC below 0.04 over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening real economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which immediately boosts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >55% of observations during recovery phases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product growth confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 295.0, elevated inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL stays above 295.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases, with a joint Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts indicate weakening residential investment and broader credit tightening, which precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not precede negative 60-day returns in >55% of slowdown periods, or the signal predictive power disappears when controlling for interest rate changes.
